Benzoin, also known as Styrax tonkinensis, is a plant native to Southeast Asia, whose bark is rich in a fragrant, sweet resin. The health benefits of Benzoin include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Benzoin include benzoin resin, styraxine, and styracin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Benzoin is commonly used in herbal preparations such as topical creams, ointments, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
